Table of Contents How complicated are the contracts? Are the pitches complex and take a lot of time? How many people are on the sales calls? Do they guarantee results? What is their pricing model? Do they retain ownership & access to all the accounts, strategies, campaigns, and material they’ve built for you? Are you considering hiring a marketing agency but worried about making the wrong choice? It’s a valid concern, considering the amount of money people have spent on the wrong agencies over the years. In this article, we’ll list out some questions that can help you identify good vs. bad agencies, ensuring you make the right decision.
How complicated are the contracts? One of the first things to consider is the complexity of the agency’s contracts. If they’re filled with legal jargon, it can be overwhelming and cause stress for the client. A straightforward contract is essential, and shackles are not. Make sure you understand everything before signing on the dotted line.
Are the pitches complex and take a lot of time? The next thing to consider is the agency’s pitches. While it’s normal for pitches to be thorough, a complex, high-pressure presentation may not be necessary. If you’re experiencing an agency that has mastered the art of selling hard, ask yourself if you can afford all this. Don’t be tempted by a fancy office and wine & dine level pitches.
How many people are on the sales calls? Following on from the pitch, consider whether you feel pressured during the sales call. You’re the customer, and you’re paying, so you shouldn’t feel like you’re being sold timeshares in Alabama. You’re looking for a partner, not an item.
Do they guarantee results? It’s easy for an agency to give estimates, but it’s another thing to guarantee results. In most cases, if an agency is guaranteeing a certain level of profitability and success, there’s likely some kind of catch. Be wary of any agency that makes lofty promises without the data to back it up.
What is their pricing model? Make sure you understand the agency’s pricing model. Are they making money from the fee, or are they making hidden money? Be aware of any additional costs or fees that may come up later.
Do they retain ownership & access to all the accounts, strategies, campaigns, and material they’ve built for you? Make sure you retain ownership and control over all accounts, campaigns, deliverables, and materials. Trust your agency to do what they need to do, but ensure you have control over your campaign and data. You paid for it, so you should possess it.
Hiring the right marketing agency can make all the difference in your business’s success. Use these questions to help you identify good vs. bad agencies and make the right choice for your business. Remember, a straightforward contract, a non-pressure sales call, and specialist knowledge are essential for a successful partnership.

Contact us or call +372 5559 9546, for personalized quote. Introduction Are you taking over a Pay per Click (PPC) campaign from someone who doesn’t know much about digital ads? It can be a daunting task, but don’t worry. We’ve got you covered with a list of common mistakes to avoid and actionable tips to optimize your PPC efforts.
Common Mistakes to Avoid Auto-apply recommendations are on Auto-applying recommendations in digital advertising is generally not a good practice for several reasons. First and foremost, auto-applying recommendations can lead to negative consequences such as decreased performance, increased costs, and poor campaign optimization.
Auto-applying recommendations can result in poor campaign optimization because the algorithm is not able to take into account the specific goals and strategies of the campaign. Recommendations may be based on general data and may not be relevant or effective for a particular campaign.
In addition, auto-applying recommendations can lead to increased costs. The algorithm may recommend strategies that require higher bids or more aggressive targeting, which can result in higher costs without necessarily improving the overall performance of the campaign.
Auto-applying recommendations can also result in decreased performance. The algorithm may recommend changes that do not align with the overall campaign strategy or that negatively impact the user experience, such as overly aggressive retargeting or poorly targeted ads. This can lead to decreased engagement, increased bounce rates, and ultimately, lower conversion rates.
Overall, it is important to approach digital advertising with a strategic mindset and carefully evaluate recommendations before implementing them. Auto-applying recommendations may seem like an easy way to improve campaign performance, but in reality, it can lead to negative consequences and poor campaign optimization.
Search campaigns set up to serve as both search and display Setting up search campaigns to serve as both search and display can be detrimental to the success of a PPC advertising campaign. While it may seem like a cost-effective way to reach a wider audience, it can actually lead to wasted ad spend and lower conversion rates.
Here are some reasons why it’s bad to use the same campaign for both search and display:
Different Targeting Options: Search ads and display ads have different targeting options, including location, demographics, and interests. Combining them in the same campaign can lead to confusion and ineffective targeting.
Different Ad Formats: Search ads and display ads also have different ad formats. Display ads are more visual and can include images and videos, while search ads are primarily text-based. Trying to create ads that work for both can lead to a lack of focus and poor performance.
Different Intent: People searching for something on Google have a specific intent, while those browsing the internet are not necessarily looking for a specific product or service. As a result, display ads are often less effective at driving conversions.
Lower Quality Scores: Combining search and display campaigns can lead to lower quality scores, which in turn can increase the cost per click and lower the ad position. This is because the ad relevance and expected click-through rate (CTR) are likely to be lower when targeting both search and display audiences.
Overall, it’s better to separate search and display campaigns to ensure that each one is optimized for its specific audience and intent. This approach can lead to better performance, higher conversion rates, and lower cost per acquisition.
Limited or no use of negative keywords, placements, etc Limited or no use of negative keywords, placements, and other targeting options can significantly harm the performance of a pay-per-click (PPC) advertising campaign. Negative keywords are terms that advertisers can exclude from their target keywords to avoid irrelevant clicks and wasted ad spend. By not using negative keywords, businesses risk showing their ads to people who are not interested in their products or services, resulting in a lower click-through rate (CTR) and wasted ad spend. …

Some synonyms of Conversion Optimization are:
Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) Conversion Improvement Conversion Maximization Conversion Enhancement Conversion Boosting The benefits that clients typically expect from a conversion optimization service Increased Conversion Rates: Clients want their website visitors to take action and become customers. Conversion optimization aims to improve the user experience and increase the percentage of visitors who convert into customers. Improved User Experience: Clients want their website to be user-friendly, easy to navigate, and engaging. Conversion optimization helps to identify and remove barriers that prevent visitors from engaging with the website and taking action. Better Return on Investment (ROI): Clients want to maximize the return on their investment in digital marketing. Conversion optimization helps to increase the effectiveness of digital marketing campaigns by improving the conversion rates of the website. Enhanced Brand Image: Clients want their brand to be seen as professional, trustworthy, and authoritative. A well-designed and optimized website can help to enhance the brand image and improve the overall perception of the business. Competitive Advantage: Clients want to stay ahead of their competitors and stand out in their industry. Conversion optimization can help to differentiate the business and create a competitive advantage by providing a better user experience and higher conversion rates. By committing to delivering these benefits through your conversion optimization service, you can help your clients to achieve their goals and grow their business.
To deliver on these benefits, you may consider implementing strategies such as A/B testing, user testing, website design improvements, copywriting improvements, and more. By analyzing user behavior and identifying areas for improvement, you can create a tailored plan to help your clients achieve their desired outcomes.
Remember to set clear expectations with your clients and communicate the progress of your work regularly. This can help to build trust and ensure that your clients are satisfied with your services. By consistently delivering on the promised benefits, you can establish yourself as a trusted and reliable provider of conversion optimization services.
To set expectations properly and deliver on the benefits of conversion optimization, you should ask your client the following questions What are your current conversion rates? What is your desired conversion rate? Who is your target audience? What are your main conversion goals (e.g., form submissions, product purchases, etc.)? What metrics are you using to track conversions (e.g., Google Analytics, heat maps, etc.)? What is your budget for conversion optimization services? Have you done any conversion optimization testing before? If so, what were the results? Are there any specific pain points or issues that you have identified with your current conversion process? How will you measure the success of the conversion optimization campaign? How involved do you want to be in the conversion optimization process (e.g., do you want to be kept informed of every change or only receive updates periodically)? By asking these questions, you can gain a better understanding of your client’s business, goals, and pain points. This information can then be used to create a customized conversion optimization plan that addresses their specific needs and helps them achieve the desired benefits.
To help your client achieve the benefits of conversion optimization, you can offer the following concrete deliverables Conversion rate audit: Start with a detailed analysis of your client’s website to identify areas for improvement and potential barriers to conversion. This will help to establish a baseline and provide insights that can be used to inform the other deliverables. User experience (UX) analysis: Once you have identified areas for improvement from the conversion rate audit, perform a UX analysis to identify specific elements that need to be improved to enhance the user experience. Conversion funnel analysis: Analyze the user journey from entry point to conversion to identify areas where users are dropping off or getting stuck. This will help to optimize the conversion funnel and improve conversion rates. Landing page optimization: Use the insights gained from the conversion rate and UX analyses to create and test landing pages with clear calls to action and messaging that resonates with the target audience. A/B testing plan: Once the landing pages have been optimized, create an A/B testing plan to determine which variations of web pages, headlines, images, and other elements produce the best conversion rates. Personalization strategy: Once the core elements of the website have been optimized, create a personalization strategy to provide a more customized experience for visitors based on their behavior, demographics, and other data points. Search Engine Marketing (SEM) is a form of digital marketing that involves the promotion of websites through paid search listings, also known as pay-per-click (PPC) advertising. SEM allows businesses to bid on specific keywords or phrases that are relevant to their products or services, and then pay a fee each time their ad is clicked. These ads are typically displayed at the top or bottom of search engine results pages (SERPs), and are marked with a “Sponsored” label to distinguish them from organic search results.
SEM differs from Search Engine Optimization (SEO) in that it involves the use of paid tactics rather than organic tactics to improve the ranking of a website on search engines. SEO involves optimizing various elements of a website, such as its content, structure, and technical elements, in order to improve its ranking on search engines like Google, Bing, and Yahoo. SEO is focused on improving the visibility and credibility of a website through organic, or unpaid, methods.
While both SEM and SEO are important for businesses and organizations looking to improve their online presence, they have different goals and approaches. SEM is often used for immediate and specific goals, such as driving traffic to a website or generating leads, while SEO is more focused on long-term, sustainable results. It is common for businesses to use both SEM and SEO as part of their overall digital marketing strategy.

Ethical concerns 4. Legal issues What is SERM and what does it stand for? SERM stands for Search Engine Reputation Management. It is the process of tracking, monitoring, and influencing the online reputation of a business or individual. This is usually done through search engine results, as well as on social media and review websites. The goal of SERM is to improve the visibility and credibility of positive content about a business or individual, while also mitigating the impact of negative content. It is an increasingly important aspect of online marketing and brand management, as the way a business or individual is perceived online can have a significant impact on their success.
How does SERM differ from traditional search engine optimization (SEO)? SEO is the process of optimizing a website to rank higher in search engine results, with the goal of increasing traffic to the site. SERM, on the other hand, is more focused on managing and improving the overall online reputation of a business or individual. While SEO and SERM can overlap in some ways, they are distinct strategies with different goals.
SEO typically involves optimizing the content and technical aspects of a website to make it more visible and attractive to search engines. This may involve things like keyword research and optimization, link building, and improving the website’s user experience.
SERM, on the other hand, is more focused on the overall online presence of a business or individual, and may involve a variety of tactics to manage and improve the way they are perceived online. This may include things like responding to reviews and mentions on social media and review websites, promoting positive content about the business or individual, and working to suppress or mitigate the impact of negative content.
In summary, SEO is focused on improving a website’s visibility and traffic through search engines, while SERM is focused on managing and improving a business or individual’s overall online reputation.

1. Responding to reviews and mentions This involves actively monitoring online review sites and social media platforms for mentions of the business or individual, and responding to both positive and negative reviews in a timely and professional manner.
2. Promoting positive content This may involve creating and sharing content that highlights the positive aspects of the business or individual, such as blog posts, articles, or social media posts.

Competition What is SERP and what does it stand for? SERP stands for Search Engine Results Page. It is the page that a search engine returns with the results of a search query. The results on a SERP can include a mix of various types of content, such as web pages, images, videos, and news articles, as well as sponsored content.
The order in which the results appear on a SERP is determined by the search engine’s algorithm, which takes into account a variety of factors, such as the relevance of the content to the search query, the authority and credibility of the source, and the user’s location and search history. The goal of a search engine is to return the most relevant and useful results to the user, so that they can find what they are looking for as quickly and easily as possible.
How do search engines determine the results that appear on a SERP? Search engines use algorithms to determine the results that appear on a SERP. These algorithms are complex and constantly evolving, and they take into account a wide variety of factors when ranking the results.
Some of the main factors that search engines consider when ranking results on a SERP include:
1. Relevance The content of the webpage must be relevant to the search query in order to rank highly.
2. Authority The website’s domain authority and the quality and credibility of its content are important factors in determining its ranking on the SERP.
3. User experience Search engines aim to provide the best possible user experience, so factors like the speed of the website, the design and layout of the page, and the ease of use all play a role in the ranking.
4. Keywords The use of relevant keywords in the content and website metadata can help to improve a website’s ranking on the SERP.
5. Links The number and quality of links pointing to a website can be an important factor in its ranking on the SERP, as they can signal to the search engine that the website is authoritative and trustworthy.
6. User location and search history The location of the user and their search history can also influence the results that appear on the SERP, as the search engine may tailor the results to the user’s specific location and interests.
By taking these and other factors into account, search engines aim to provide the most relevant and useful results to the user.
What are the different types of results that can appear on a SERP? There are several types of results that can appear on a SERP:
1. Web pages These are the most common type of results on a SERP, and they include the content and pages of websites.
2. Images When a user searches for images, the SERP may include a selection of images that are relevant to the search query.
3. Videos When a user searches for videos, the SERP may include a selection of videos that are relevant to the search query.
4. News articles When a user searches for news articles, the SERP may include a selection of articles from news websites that are relevant to the search query.
5. Local results When a user searches for a local business or service, the SERP may include a map and a list of local businesses that are relevant to the search query.
6. Advertisements The SERP may also include sponsored results, which are paid advertisements that are designed to appear at the top or bottom of the page. These advertisements are labeled as “Ad” or “Sponsored” to distinguish them from the organic results.

Осмотрев сайт и магазин мы можем утверждать, что существует много проблем в области поисковой оптимизации вызванных некачественной разработкой и изначально непродуманной структурой.
Для того, чтобы люди попадали на сайт страницы должны быть “проиндексированы” Google. Если поисковому роботу, который индексирует страницы, не нравится как страница сделана, как организован материал на странице или он видит ошибки - индексирования не будет. Это тревожный и плохой сигнал, свидетельствующий об однозначных проблемах с сайтом.
Как узнать сколько страниц проиндексировано в Google? https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aiproff.ee
Критически мало!
Проблемы сайта На сайте iproff, мягко говоря, беспорядок. В админке wordpress видно, что разработано тяп-ляп.
Подключено много бессмысленных, ни для чего не нужных плагинов.
Плагины не обновлены, что создает риск безопасности.
Инсталляция сочится ошибками.
Тема оформления изготовленная неким Dmitri gomedia.ee, не отвечает стандартам и содержит ошибки.
Примечательно, что при переходе на сайт разработчика https://gomedia.ee/ броузер ругается:
Warning: Potential Security Risk Ahead. Firefox detected a potential security threat and did not continue to gomedia.ee. If you visit this site, attackers could try to steal information like your passwords, emails, or credit card details.
Отсутствует вменяемая структура и содержимое
категорий тагов страниц постов Все выведено в одну заглавную страницу, что очень неверно с точки зрения поисковой оптимизации и привлечения трафика из Google.
Проблемы магазина Отсутствует возможность прямо сейчас заплатить кредитной картой, что очень понижает количество клиентов. Четверо из пяти человек предпочли бы заплатить прямо сейчас, а не заморачиваться с переводом денег на счет. Это очень понижает доход магазина.
Доставка не интегрирована с оплатой. Лучше печатать этикетки самим, с готовыми данными клиента, автоматически генерируемым трекинг-кодом, а не делать все вручную, как сейчас.
Используется любительская тема оформления Divi, со встроенным конструктором страниц для новичков.
Неверная структура локализованных URL, с избыточной вложенностью, которая не позволит высоко ранжировать магазин
<link rel="alternate" href="https://shop.iproff.ee/" hreflang="et" /> <link rel="alternate" href="https://shop.iproff.ee/en/online-shop/" hreflang="en" /> <link rel="alternate" href="https://shop.iproff.ee/ru/shop/" hreflang="ru" /> Сотни фундаментальных ошибок, показывающих о полном непонимании принципов поисковой оптимизации,
например:
Важный мета-тэг description, на эстонской версии магазина, содержит описание на трех языках (а нужно только на одном, эстонском) . Google ненавидит подобное.
<meta name="description" content="Ekraani kiirvahetus Tallinnas - iPhone ja Apple seadmed, Быстрая починка экранов iPhone и Apple устройств в пределах Таллинна, Quick fix of iPhone and Apple device screens within Tallinn," /> важнейший тэг title вообще не содержит ничего полезного, а это самое главное место.
<title>E-pood - iProff</title> Разработчик установил любительский плагин All in One SEO 4.2.0, которым не умеет пользоваться. Плагин ЗАНОВО, повторно прописал в страницу мета-тэг description с уже другими значениями. Это страшный грех в глазах Google,он считает подобное спамом и удаляет сайт из своей базы, и трафика не видать.
<meta name="description" content="E-pood Watch 5S SE 6 6 Plus 6S 6S Plus 7 7 Plus 8 8 Plus X XS XR XS MAX 11 11 PRO 12 12 PRO 12 Pro Max 12 mini Allahindlusega tooted Silikonrihm Sport (erinevad värvid)Sale!€14,99 – €19,99 Silikonrihm (erinevad värvid)Sale!€10,99 – €14,99 Apple Watch Milanese rihm (erinevad värvid)Sale!€14,99 – €24,99 Apple AirPods" /> Тревожный код! AdSense - это система от Google, которая позволяет выводить на страницах рекламу, и если на нее кликнет посетитель страницы, то вебмастер получит оплату за клик. Ставить эту систему в магазин не имеет никакого смысла и выгоды для владельца магазина, потому что она будет уводить потенциального клиента со страницы. Но для вебмастера или разработчика смысл есть и довольно большой. Можно тайно от владельца демонстрировать рекламу, по ключевым словам типа Apple, и класть себе в карман доход, не взирая на огромный вред наносимый владельцу магазина. На сайте присутствует код выводящий рекламу Adsense!

content:
Технический SEO анализ проблем
1. URL URL адреса длиной более 80 символов негативно влияют на индексацию и SEO ранжирование. На сайте megateh.ee найдено 669 слишком длинных URL адресов.
Структура URL мешает Google высоко ранжировать, например вот это категорически плохо и неверно: www.megateh.fi/fi/uusimmat-tuotteemme
2. Cсылки Слишком много бессмысленных ссылок на другие страницы сайта Внутренняя перелинковка очень плохая. Слишком много ссылок на другие ненужные страницы. На 3108 страницах аж ДВЕСТИ ТЫСЯЧ ссылок на них самих.
Поломанные ссылки. На сайте megateh.ee найдено 538 ссылок, которые ведут в никуда, и этим сбивают роботов Google, и это понижает позицию в выдаче.
Страницы на которых слишком много ссылок На сайте megateh.ee найдено 1725 страниц, на которых слишком много ссылок на другие страницы. Это вводит в заблуждение Google, и понижает позицию в выдаче.
Dofollow внешние ссылки По ссылкам наружу из сайта утекает т.н. Link Juice или авторитет. Их нужно ставить с большой осторожностью и вниманием. На сайте megateh.ee найдено 5192 внешние ссылки, что примерно на 5150 больше, чем следует :)
3. Изображения Отсутствующие изображения На сайте megateh.ee найдено 72 отсутствующих или поломанных изображений, что является грехом в глазах Google.
Пустой alt текст Альтернативный текст для изображения - это очень важный для Google элемент ранжирования. Он должен содержать важную информацию о картинке, лучше всего связанную с названием товара. На сайте megateh.ee найдено 311 изображений без прописанного ALT текста.
Бессмысленный alt и title текст Во многих товаров в Альтернативном тексте и атрибуте TITLE указано название файла, что никак не помогает Google, и раздражает его. Например:
URL продукта: https://www.megateh.ee/tooted/partizan-videovalve-ip-komplektid/partizan-2mp-outdoor-wi-fi-komplekt-ip-39-4xcam-1xnvr HTML код изображения:
<img class="pic" src="/files/products/00/53/42/outdoor-wireless-kit-pack_store.jpg" alt="outdoor-wireless-kit-pack_store.jpg" title="outdoor-wireless-kit-pack_store.jpg"> Текущий неправильный ALT text: outdoor-wireless-kit-pack_store.jpg
Текущий неправильный, бессмысленный и дублирующий TITLE text: outdoor-wireless-kit-pack_store.jpg
Правильный ALT: 2MP Outdoor Wi-Fi komplekt IP-39 4xCAM + 1xNVR
Правильный TITLE: IP Videovalve - Outdoor wireless - Partizan
Названия изображений товаров Очень важно чтобы названия изображений содержали ключевые слова из названия товара. На сайте многие изображения названы неверно. Например:
URL продукта: https://www.megateh.ee/tooted/partizan-videovalve-ip-komplektid/partizan-2mp-outdoor-wi-fi-komplekt-ip-39-4xcam-1xnvr
Название продукта: “Partizan 2MP Outdoor Wi-Fi komplekt IP-39 4xCAM + 1xNVR”
Проблемы:
URL изображения содержит излишне глубокую структуру вложенных папок: /files/products/00/53/42/outdoor-wireless-kit-pack_store.jpg
Текущее неправильное название заглавной картинки: outdoor-wireless-kit-pack_store.jpg
Как должна была бы правильно называться: partizan-2mp-outdoor-wi-fi-komplekt-ip-39-4xcam-1xnvr.jpg
URL продукта, Заголовок продукта, название изображений товара должны содержать одинаковые, хорошо структурированные ключевые слова
4. Структура категорий Лучше всего поддерживать древовидную структуру инфраструктуры, где под-категории имеют только одну родительскую категорию. Надо стремиться к трехуровневой древовидной структуре: мета-категория → категория-ветвь → категория-лист
На сайте полный беспорядок в структуре, который не дает Google правильно проиндексировать и высоко ранжировать. Например:
Мета категория: Videovalvesüsteemid - слишком длинное название, люди вряд ли будут так искать, правильнее было бы назвать Videovalve
URL категории: /tooted/videovalve - правильнее было бы сделать категорию так: /product-category/videovalve или /videovalve
дальше, в этой мета-категории есть категория-ветвь “IP kaamerad”, URL которой критически неверно называется /tooted/valvekaamerad. Теряется ключевое слово в самом важном месте. Правильнее было бы назвать /videovalve/ip-kaamerad
дальше, в этой мета-категории есть категория-ветвь с названием производителя PARTIZAN, что уже неверно, т.к. производитель - это Атрибут товара, а не категория-ветвь. URL производителя /tooted/partizan, а должен был бы быть например /manufacturer/partizan
Но это еще не все. Ненужная категория-ветвь PARTIZAN содержит еще одну дублирующую подкатегорию “IP kaamerad”. URL /tooted/partizan-ip-valvekaamerad, что вообще запутывает Google и не содержит ни одного важного мета ключевого слова
5. Атрибуты товаров Важно чтобы атрибуты (свойства) продуктов не представляли из себя просто текстовые строки в описании товара. Атрибуты должны быть отдельными, кликабельными элементами по которым можно осуществлять:
поиск индексацию группировку Для этого необходима правильная структура базы данных товаров. Вот отличный пример как должно быть:
https://partizanstore.eu/products/wifi-ip-39-4-cam/#tab-additional_information
Название атрибута: Image sensor
Значение атрибута: 1/2.8" Full HD CMOS …

Our search engine optimization services include a personalized strategy, cutting-edge technology and a smart SEO professionals backed up by 15 years of experience.
We work to increase revenue for our clients and provide every piece of technologies and services required for your business to grow with SEO. Are you tired of your website languishing on page 10 of Google’s search results? Are you ready to attract more qualified traffic and increase your online revenue? If so, it’s time to invest in search engine optimization (SEO) services from our agency.
What is SEO? SEO is the practice of optimizing your website and its content in order to rank higher in search engine results pages (SERPs) and earn more relevant traffic. It’s an essential part of any digital marketing strategy, as it helps ensure that your website is visible to the right audience at the right time.
Our SEO experts has a wealth of experience in helping businesses just like yours improve their online visibility and drive more traffic to their websites. We use a data-driven approach to identify the keywords and phrases that your target audience is searching for, and then optimize your website’s content and structure to rank for those terms.
In addition to on-page optimization, we also focus on off-page factors such as link building and social media engagement. These efforts help to establish your website as a credible and trustworthy source, further improving your search rankings.
But SEO isn’t just about driving traffic – it’s also about converting that traffic into paying customers. That’s why we work closely with you to understand your business goals and develop a customized SEO strategy that delivers the results you need.
If you are ready to take your online presence to the next level and start seeing a real return on your investment:
Contact us today to learn more and schedule a consultation.
SEO process The SEO process begins with a thorough analysis of your website and your competitors.
We identify any technical issues that may be hindering your search engine performance and work to fix them.
We also conduct keyword research to identify the phrases and terms that your target audience is using to find products or services like yours.
Once we have a solid understanding of your website and your goals, we develop a customized SEO plan to improve your search engine rankings. This may include on-page optimization, such as optimizing titles and meta descriptions, as well as off-page tactics like link building and local SEO.
In addition to improving your search engine rankings, we are good in conversion rate optimization (CRO). We work to not only bring more traffic to your website, but to also ensure that those visitors are taking desired actions, such as making a purchase or filling out a contact form.
